title: cyathea gregaria
intro: Cyathea gregaria, also known as the black tree fern, is a species of fern that is native to eastern Australia and New Zealand. It is a popular ornamental plant due to its dark green fronds and ability to tolerate a range of conditions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of certain phenolic compounds. Cyathea gregaria has been used to treat a variety of health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and gastrointestinal problems, and can be prepared in herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal purposes.
